# tables

Table collection with Table/Cell elements.

# tables.TableCollection { #hwpapi.collections.tables.TableCollection }

```python
collections.tables.TableCollection(app)
```

``doc.tables`` — tables in document order.

# tables.Table { #hwpapi.collections.tables.Table }

```python
collections.tables.Table(app, ctrl, index)
```

Value object for a single table control.

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [caption](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Table.caption) | Caption text, or an empty string if the table has none. |
| [cols](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Table.cols) | Number of columns in the table (0 if unknown). |
| [name](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Table.name) | Best-effort display name — caption when present, else ordinal. |
| [rows](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Table.rows) | Number of rows in the table (0 if unknown). |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [cell](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Table.cell) | Return a :class:`Cell` for ``(row, col)`` (0-indexed). |

### cell { #hwpapi.collections.tables.Table.cell }

```python
collections.tables.Table.cell(row, col)
```

Return a :class:`Cell` for ``(row, col)`` (0-indexed).

# tables.Cell { #hwpapi.collections.tables.Cell }

```python
collections.tables.Cell(app, table, row, col)
```

Value object for a single table cell.

Phase 4 surface: ``.text``, ``.address`` (A1-style), ``.select()``.
Does not own a COM object — addresses are passed to the HWP engine
on demand.

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [address](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Cell.address) | A1-style address (e.g. ``"B3"``). |
| [text](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Cell.text) | Best-effort cell text. Selects the cell, reads the saved-block |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [select](#hwpapi.collections.tables.Cell.select) | Navigate the caret to this cell. Selects the owning table first, |

### select { #hwpapi.collections.tables.Cell.select }

```python
collections.tables.Cell.select()
```

Navigate the caret to this cell. Selects the owning table first,
then uses ``SetCellAddr`` / ``TableCellBlock`` navigation. Returns
``True`` on success, ``False`` on any COM error.
